title: Downgrade code to support python3.4
body:
* Added tox configuration to test python 3.4, 3.5 and 3.6.
  Tox testing not enabled on every PR
* Updated MANIFEST.ini to support tox
* Correct comments

Fixes #1641

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body: Why are we doing this? Python 3.6 is available for RHEL/CentOS through EPEL and SCL...
LGTM, but the tests don't appear to be happy.

FWIW: I've already got SCL enabled for both RHEL and CentOS Cirrus-CI VM images.  It would be really easy for me to add it (for future testing).

Ref: https://www.softwarecollections.org/en/scls/rhscl/rh-python36/

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Can someone answer @Conan-Kudo Question, why go to such an old version of python?

One of my friends who works in a company of China told me they're using python34, I was surprised too. They're using a mirror source of:
`wget -O /etc/yum.repos.d/epel.repo http://mirrors.aliyun.com/repo/epel-7.repo`. So I installed python34 from that and wanted to see what would happen with pypodman. @Conan-Kudo @rhatdan
Fedora EPEL will be rebuilding everything for `python36` later this month and retiring `python34` soon... There's an upcoming EPEL VFAD for getting this done next week, I think.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1638) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
